concept_name: Torque on a Current Loop in a Magnetic Field (τ = μ × B)
source_book: DC Pandey Vol 2 Ch.26 §26.6 — table-of-contents reference only; teaching authored from first principles. Diamond #3 of the magnetism chapter (phase M2 per docs/MAGNETISM_ARCHITECTURE.md §10, archetype C — closed-loop rotational dynamics). The atomic concept being taught is that a current loop in a uniform B feels zero net force but a non-zero torque τ = μ × B, where μ = NIA points perpendicular to the loop face (RHR-derived from current direction). The four most common student errors targeted here: (a) believing magnetic force does work on the loop, (b) believing the net force on a rotating loop is non-zero, (c) believing torque is maximum when μ ∥ B (alignment), (d) confusing the RHR for μ with the RHR for the current's own field. Diamond #3 establishes archetype-C renderer primitives (rectangular loop, force-pair animation, μ vector through loop face, τ vector along rotation axis, oscillation animation, loop↔bar-magnet swap) so vibration_magnetometer, moving_coil_galvanometer, and bar_magnet_in_uniform_field can author against them via the M3 patterns library. Conforms to Rule 20 M1-M6 exception: ships conceptual-only; mode_overrides.board deferred to M7, .competitive deferred to M8.

real_world_anchor.primary: Picture the analog galvanometer in your school physics lab — the brass-framed instrument with a thin needle that flicks across a paper scale when current flows. Inside that brass case is a tiny rectangular coil suspended in the field of two permanent magnets. When you connect a battery, current flows through the coil and the same τ = μ × B torque you'll see in a moment swings the needle. The bigger the current, the bigger the torque, the further the needle deflects against the restoring spring. Every micrometer reading on that scale is a torque measurement.
real_world_anchor.secondary: Picture a ceiling fan motor in a Bengaluru summer apartment. Inside the white plastic housing is a rotor wound with current-carrying coils sitting in the field of the stator's permanent magnets. Each half-rotation, the commutator flips the current direction so the torque on the rotor stays in the same rotational sense. That continuous τ = μ × B torque, flipped at the right moment, is what keeps the blades spinning at 350 RPM all afternoon.
real_world_anchor.tertiary: Picture a moving-coil microphone on stage at an IIT-Bombay Mood Indigo concert. The singer's voice vibrates a thin diaphragm, which vibrates a tiny coil sitting in a permanent-magnet field. Each oscillation of the coil is a τ = μ × B torque cycle. The induced EMF reads out the audio signal. The microphone is just a galvanometer running backwards.

N.name: Number of turns in the coil

L_side.name: Side length of the square loop

B.name: Magnitude of the uniform external field

tau_magnitude.formula: N * I * (L_side * L_side) * B * sin(theta_deg * PI / 180)

formulas.tau_vector: τ = μ × B — the vector torque is the cross product of the loop's magnetic moment with the external field.
formulas.tau_magnitude: τ = μ B sin θ — scalar magnitude; θ is the angle between μ and B; τ is maximum at θ = 90° and zero at θ = 0° or 180°.
formulas.mu_definition: μ = N I A — the magnetic moment of the loop. Direction: perpendicular to the loop face, RHR (curl fingers along current, thumb gives μ).
formulas.net_force_zero: F_top + F_bottom + F_left + F_right = 0 — opposite sides feel equal-and-opposite forces; the loop translates nowhere.
formulas.force_per_side: F = I (L × B) — force on each current-carrying segment of length L.
formulas.equilibrium_stable: θ = 0° (μ ∥ B) — stable equilibrium; small perturbation returns to alignment.
formulas.equilibrium_unstable: θ = 180° (μ anti-parallel to B) — unstable; any perturbation grows.
formulas.no_work_on_loop: Pure magnetic torque is conservative on the loop face. KE oscillates symmetrically about θ = 0 with no average gain — magnetic field does no NET work on the rotating loop.

constraints: B must be uniform across the loop. Non-uniform B adds a net force (gradient term) and changes the dynamics.
Loop is rigid — wire is non-deformable; geometry preserved during rotation.
Rotation axis is fixed by the geometry — the axis perpendicular to BOTH the field-aligned sides and the τ vector.
θ is measured from μ to B (not B to μ). Sign convention: positive θ → restoring torque toward θ = 0.
τ = μ × B holds for ANY planar loop shape (rectangle, circle, triangle); the rectangular case is the easy derivation, not a special case.
No emf back-reaction included here — induction belongs to the next chapter.

aha_moment.statement: ΣF can be zero while Στ is non-zero — rotation without translation.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: Watch STATE_3 → STATE_4: ΣF arrow shrinks to a literal zero badge, yet the τ vector appears and the loop starts to rotate. The two facts are simultaneously true.

STATE_1.title: Setup — a rectangular loop sits at θ = 90° in a uniform B field

scene_composition.text: A rectangular current loop in uniform B (θ = 90°)

Blue arrows = B (uniform field)
Yellow loop = current-carrying coil
Orange dots = current flowing around coil
Watch the dots — they show which way I flows.

Question:
What happens to the loop?
Same setup as the school-lab galvanometer — coil suspended in a permanent-magnet field.

tts_sentences.text_en: Setup. Blue arrows are the uniform magnetic field B.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Setup. Blue arrows uniform magnetic field B हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: Setup. Blue arrows అంటే uniform magnetic field B.

tts_sentences.text_en: The yellow rectangle is a current loop. Watch the orange dots — they flow around the loop showing you exactly which way the current moves.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Yellow rectangle एक current loop है। Orange dots देखो — वे loop के चारों तरफ flow करते हैं और exactly बताते हैं कि current किस direction में move करती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Yellow rectangle అనేది ఒక current loop. Orange dots చూడండి — అవి loop చుట్టూ flow అవుతాయి, current ఏ direction లో move అవుతుందో exactly చూపిస్తాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The loop sits at theta equals ninety — its face is parallel to magnetic field B.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Loop theta equals ninety पर है — इसका face magnetic field B के parallel है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Loop theta equals ninety వద్ద ఉంది — దాని face magnetic field B కు parallel గా ఉంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Question. What does the loop do? Does it slide? Does it spin? Does it just sit there?
tts_sentences.text_hi: Question. Loop क्या करती है? क्या वह slide करती है? क्या वह spin करती है? या वह बस वहाँ बैठी रहती है?
tts_sentences.text_te: Question. Loop ఏమి చేస్తుంది? అది slide అవుతుందా? spin అవుతుందా? లేక అక్కడే ఉండిపోతుందా?

STATE_2.title: Force on one side — F = I L × B reveals on the left side

scene_composition.text: Predict, then reveal: F on the LEFT side

Watch the dots on the LEFT side.
Current flows UP.
B points to the right.
Predict: which way is the force?

See the guide (top right):
I arrow = up, B arrow = right
→ F = I × B points INTO the page.
F₁ into the page. ✓
F₁ = I · L_side · B. For our defaults: F₁ = 0.5 · 0.10 · 0.1 = 0.005 N.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the orange dots on the left side of the loop. They flow upward. Current on the left side is going up.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Loop के left side पर orange dots देखो। वे ऊपर की तरफ flow कर रहे हैं। Left side पर current ऊपर जा रही है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Loop యొక్క left side పై orange dots చూడండి. అవి upward గా flow అవుతున్నాయి. Left side పై current పైకి వెళ్తోంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Predict before I show you. Current up, field pointing right. Which way is the force? Take three seconds.
tts_sentences.text_hi: मैं दिखाने से पहले predict करो। Current ऊपर, field right की तरफ। Force किस direction में है? तीन seconds लो।
tts_sentences.text_te: నేను చూపించే ముందే predict చేయండి. Current పైకి, field right వైపు. Force ఏ direction లో ఉంది? మూడు seconds తీసుకోండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Look at the guide in the upper right. Three arrows: current I pointing up, magnetic field B pointing right, and force F equals I cross B.
tts_sentences.text_hi: ऊपर दाएं guide देखो। तीन arrows: current I ऊपर, magnetic field B right, और force F equals I cross B।
tts_sentences.text_te: Upper right లో guide చూడండి. మూడు arrows: current I పైకి, magnetic field B right వైపు, మరియు force F equals I cross B.

tts_sentences.text_en: Force F points into the page — away from you. That is the cross product: thumb up, fingers right, palm faces away. F-one into the page.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Force F page के अंदर point करता है — तुमसे दूर। यही cross product है: thumb ऊपर, fingers right, palm दूर की तरफ। F-one page के अंदर।
tts_sentences.text_te: Force F page లోపలికి point అవుతుంది — మీ నుండి దూరంగా. ఇదే cross product: thumb పైకి, fingers right వైపు, palm దూరంగా. F-one page లోపలికి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Magnitude: current I times L times B. For our defaults, five thousandths of a newton.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Magnitude: current I times L times B। हमारे default values के लिए, five thousandths of a newton।
tts_sentences.text_te: Magnitude: current I times L times B. మన default values కోసం, five thousandths of a newton.

STATE_3.title: Force on the opposite side — F₂ is anti-parallel; ΣF = 0

scene_composition.text: Force on the RIGHT side — and net force

Watch the dots on the RIGHT side.
Current flows DOWN.
B still points right.
Predict F before looking at the guide.

Guide shows: I down, B right
→ F = I × B points OUT of the page.
F₂ and F₁ are anti-parallel. ✓
ΣF = F₁ + F₂ = 0. The loop will NOT translate. Anywhere.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now the right side. Watch the orange dots — they flow downward. Current on the right is going down.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब right side। Orange dots देखो — वे नीचे flow कर रहे हैं। Right side पर current नीचे जा रही है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు right side. Orange dots చూడండి — అవి downward గా flow అవుతున్నాయి. Right side పై current కిందికి వెళ్తోంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Predict again before the guide shows you. Current down, field right. Which way is the force?
tts_sentences.text_hi: Guide दिखाने से पहले फिर से predict करो। Current नीचे, field right। Force किस तरफ है?
tts_sentences.text_te: Guide చూపించే ముందే మళ్ళీ predict చేయండి. Current కిందికి, field right వైపు. Force ఏ వైపుకు?

tts_sentences.text_en: Look at the guide. Current I arrow down, magnetic field B arrow right. Force F equals I cross B — out of the page this time.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Guide देखो। current I arrow नीचे, magnetic field B arrow right। force F equals I cross B — इस बार page के बाहर।
tts_sentences.text_te: Guide చూడండి. current I arrow కిందికి, magnetic field B arrow right వైపు. force F equals I cross B — ఈసారి page బయటికి.

tts_sentences.text_en: F-two is out of the page. Opposite direction to F-one. Same magnitude. Add them — net force is zero.
tts_sentences.text_hi: F-two page के बाहर है। F-one के opposite direction में। Same magnitude। दोनों को add करो — net force zero है।
tts_sentences.text_te: F-two page బయట ఉంది. F-one కు opposite direction లో. Same magnitude. రెండూ add చేయండి — net force zero.

tts_sentences.text_en: But notice — the forces act on DIFFERENT lines of action. Left side and right side. That asymmetry is about to matter.
tts_sentences.text_hi: लेकिन ध्यान दो — forces DIFFERENT lines of action पर act कर रहे हैं। Left side और right side। यह asymmetry अब important होने वाली है।
tts_sentences.text_te: కానీ గమనించండి — forces DIFFERENT lines of action పై act చేస్తున్నాయి. Left side మరియు right side. ఈ asymmetry ఇప్పుడు important అవుతుంది.

STATE_4.title: Torque emerges — two opposite forces on different lines = a couple

scene_composition.text: A couple → rotation, not translation

Two equal-and-opposite forces
on different lines of action
= COUPLE

ΣF = 0  ✓  no translation
Στ ≠ 0  →  rotation begins
Zero net force and non-zero net torque are simultaneously true. This is the heart of rotational dynamics.

tts_sentences.text_en: Two forces. Equal magnitude. Opposite direction. Different lines of action. What does that combination do?
tts_sentences.text_hi: दो forces। Equal magnitude। Opposite direction। Different lines of action। यह combination क्या करता है?
tts_sentences.text_te: రెండు forces. Equal magnitude. Opposite direction. Different lines of action. ఈ combination ఏమి చేస్తుంది?

tts_sentences.text_en: Take three seconds. Picture the loop. Picture the forces.
tts_sentences.text_hi: तीन seconds लो। Loop की picture बनाओ। Forces की picture बनाओ।
tts_sentences.text_te: మూడు seconds తీసుకోండి. Loop ని imagine చేయండి. Forces ని imagine చేయండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: It is a couple. A couple produces rotation with no translation. The loop will spin about the vertical axis through its centre.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह एक couple है। Couple rotation produce करता है बिना translation के। Loop अपने centre से गुजरने वाले vertical axis के बारे में spin करेगी।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది ఒక couple. Couple అనేది translation లేకుండా rotation ఉత్పన్నం చేస్తుంది. Loop దాని centre గుండా వెళ్ళే vertical axis చుట్టూ spin అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Sum of forces is zero. Sum of torques is not zero. Both statements are true at the same time.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Sum of forces zero है। Sum of torques zero नहीं है। दोनों बातें एक साथ सच हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: Sum of forces zero. Sum of torques zero కాదు. రెండు statements ఒకే సమయంలో నిజమే.

tts_sentences.text_en: This is the heart of rotational dynamics. Forces decide translation. Torques decide rotation.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यही rotational dynamics का heart है। Forces translation decide करते हैं। Torques rotation decide करते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇదే rotational dynamics యొక్క heart. Forces translation decide చేస్తాయి. Torques rotation decide చేస్తాయి.

misconception_watch.belief: If ΣF = 0, the loop must be in equilibrium and cannot move at all.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Translational equilibrium ≠ rotational equilibrium. A couple has ΣF = 0 but Στ ≠ 0 — the loop rotates but does not translate.

STATE_5.title: The dipole moment μ = NIA — direction by RHR through the loop face

scene_composition.text: The magnetic moment μ = N I A

μ is a VECTOR.
Magnitude: N · I · A
Direction: ⊥ loop face
(RHR: curl fingers along I,
 thumb gives μ).

μ-arrow length scales as N · I · A.
Double the turns → double μ.
Double the current → double μ.
Double the area → double μ.
μ unifies the loop with the bar magnet. A current loop IS a magnetic dipole. STATE 7 will make the analogy visual.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now name the thing that makes a loop magnetic. It is called the magnetic moment, written as mu.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब उस चीज़ को नाम दो जो loop को magnetic बनाती है। इसे magnetic moment कहते हैं, mu लिखा जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు loop ని magnetic గా చేసే దానికి పేరు చెప్పండి. దీన్ని magnetic moment అంటారు, mu అని రాస్తారు.

tts_sentences.text_en: Magnitude: N times I times A. N is the number of turns, I is the current, A is the loop area.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Magnitude: N times I times A। N turns की संख्या है, I current है, A loop का area है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Magnitude: N times I times A. N అంటే turns సంఖ్య, I అంటే current, A అంటే loop యొక్క area.

tts_sentences.text_en: Direction: perpendicular to the loop face. Right-hand rule: curl your fingers in the direction of current. Your thumb points along mu.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Direction: loop face के perpendicular। Right-hand rule: अपनी fingers को current की direction में curl करो। Thumb mu के साथ point करेगा।
tts_sentences.text_te: Direction: loop face కు perpendicular. Right-hand rule: మీ fingers ని current direction లో curl చేయండి. Thumb mu వైపు point అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the mu-arrow size. It scales with N times I times A. Bigger turns or bigger current means bigger mu.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Mu-arrow की size देखो। यह N times I times A के साथ scale होती है। Bigger turns या bigger current मतलब bigger mu।
tts_sentences.text_te: Mu-arrow యొక్క size చూడండి. అది N times I times A తో scale అవుతుంది. ఎక్కువ turns లేదా ఎక్కువ current అంటే bigger mu.

STATE_6.title: θ sweeps 0 → 180° — τ traces a sine

scene_composition.text: Watch θ sweep 0° → 180° — τ traces a sine

τ starts at 0 (θ = 0°)
rises as θ rises
peaks somewhere mid-way
falls back to 0 (θ = 180°)

Functional form:
τ = μ B sin θ

This is a SINE curve.
You will guess where it peaks in the next state.
Watch only. No sliders yet. The interactive sandbox arrives at the very end.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch only. Theta sweeps from zero to one-eighty degrees and back, on a ten-second cycle.
tts_sentences.text_hi: सिर्फ देखो। Theta zero से one-eighty degrees तक और वापस sweep करता है, दस seconds के cycle पर।
tts_sentences.text_te: కేవలం చూడండి. Theta zero నుండి one-eighty degrees వరకు మరియు వెనక్కి sweep అవుతుంది, పది seconds cycle లో.

tts_sentences.text_en: Notice the torque arrow. It starts at zero, grows as theta grows, peaks somewhere in the middle, and returns to zero.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Torque arrow ध्यान से देखो। यह zero से शुरू होता है, theta बढ़ने के साथ बढ़ता है, बीच में कहीं peak करता है, और zero पर वापस आ जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Torque arrow గమనించండి. అది zero నుండి మొదలవుతుంది, theta పెరిగే కొద్దీ పెరుగుతుంది, మధ్యలో ఎక్కడో peak అవుతుంది, మరియు zero కి తిరిగి వస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: That shape is a sine. Tau equals mu B sine theta. Keep watching — you will guess where the peak is in the next state.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह shape sine है। Tau equals mu B sine theta। देखते रहो — अगले state में तुम peak कहाँ है यह guess करोगे।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఆ shape ఒక sine. Tau equals mu B sine theta. చూస్తూ ఉండండి — తర్వాతి state లో peak ఎక్కడ ఉందో మీరు guess చేస్తారు.

STATE_7.title: Where is τ maximum? — predict before you see

scene_composition.text: At what angle is τ MAXIMUM?

Common wrong guess:
θ = 0°  (μ ∥ B)

"Aligned means biggest, right?"

Actual answer: θ = 90°.
τ = μ B sin 90° = μ B

Cross product is biggest when the two vectors are PERPENDICULAR, not parallel.
τ = μ × B. Parallel → sin 0 = 0 → τ = 0. Perpendicular → sin 90 = 1 → τ = max.

tts_sentences.text_en: The loop is now frozen at theta equals ninety degrees. Look at the torque arrow — fully extended.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Loop अब theta equals ninety degrees पर frozen है। Torque arrow देखो — पूरी तरह extended है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Loop ఇప్పుడు theta equals ninety degrees వద్ద frozen గా ఉంది. Torque arrow చూడండి — పూర్తిగా extended గా ఉంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Predict first. At what angle does the torque reach its maximum? Three seconds.
tts_sentences.text_hi: पहले predict करो। Torque किस angle पर maximum पहुँचता है? तीन seconds।
tts_sentences.text_te: ముందు predict చేయండి. Torque ఏ angle వద్ద maximum కి చేరుతుంది? మూడు seconds.

tts_sentences.text_en: Many students say theta zero — where mu is parallel to magnetic field B. That feels right. It is wrong. The answer is ninety degrees.
tts_sentences.text_hi: बहुत से students theta zero कहते हैं — जहाँ mu, magnetic field B के parallel है। यह सही लगता है। लेकिन यह गलत है। Answer ninety degrees है।
tts_sentences.text_te: చాలా మంది students theta zero అంటారు — ఇక్కడ mu, magnetic field B కు parallel గా ఉంటుంది. అది సరిగ్గా అనిపిస్తుంది. కానీ అది తప్పు. Answer ninety degrees.

tts_sentences.text_en: Because tau is a cross product. Cross products are biggest when the two vectors are perpendicular, smallest when they are parallel.
tts_sentences.text_hi: क्योंकि tau एक cross product है। Cross products सबसे बड़े होते हैं जब दो vectors perpendicular होते हैं, सबसे छोटे जब वे parallel होते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఎందుకంటే tau ఒక cross product. రెండు vectors perpendicular గా ఉన్నప్పుడు cross products అత్యధికంగా ఉంటాయి, parallel గా ఉన్నప్పుడు అత్యల్పంగా ఉంటాయి.

misconception_watch.belief: Torque is maximum when μ ∥ B because that is when the dipole is most aligned.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Alignment means τ = 0, not τ_max. τ = μ × B is a cross product — it is biggest when μ ⊥ B.

STATE_8.title: θ = 0° is stable — nudge it, it returns

scene_composition.text: θ = 0° — STABLE equilibrium

μ ∥ B, τ = 0.
The loop sits still.

Nudge it →
the torque points BACK toward θ = 0°.
It returns. It oscillates.

Same as a compass needle:
resting pose is north.
Push it east → it swings back.

This is why compasses work.

tts_sentences.text_en: The loop now oscillates around theta zero. Watch the swing — it always returns to mu parallel to magnetic field B.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Loop अब theta zero के चारों तरफ oscillate कर रही है। Swing देखो — यह हमेशा mu parallel to magnetic field B पर वापस आती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Loop ఇప్పుడు theta zero చుట్టూ oscillate చేస్తోంది. Swing చూడండి — అది ఎప్పుడూ mu parallel to magnetic field B కి తిరిగి వస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: This is stable equilibrium. Nudge the loop — the torque points back toward theta zero. Like a marble in a bowl.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह stable equilibrium है। Loop को nudge करो — torque theta zero की तरफ वापस point करता है। जैसे bowl में marble।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది stable equilibrium. Loop ని nudge చేయండి — torque theta zero వైపు వెనక్కి point అవుతుంది. Bowl లో marble లాంటిది.

tts_sentences.text_en: A compass needle behaves exactly this way. Resting pose is north. Push it east, it swings back. Same physics.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Compass needle ठीक इसी तरह behave करती है। Resting pose north है। उसे east की तरफ push करो, वह वापस swing करती है। Same physics।
tts_sentences.text_te: Compass needle సరిగ్గా ఇలాగే behave చేస్తుంది. Resting pose north. దాన్ని east వైపు push చేయండి, అది వెనక్కి swing అవుతుంది. Same physics.

STATE_9.title: θ = 180° is unstable — nudge it, the loop flips

scene_composition.text: θ = 180° — UNSTABLE equilibrium

μ anti-parallel to B, τ = 0.

Looks stationary — but any
tiny nudge points τ AWAY
from θ = 180°.
The loop flips all the way through to θ = 0°.

Like a pencil balanced on its tip.
Mathematically allowed.
Practically: any disturbance
grows. It cannot hold.

Real loops always end up at θ = 0°.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now watch what happens at theta one-eighty. The loop starts there. Torque is zero — it should be in equilibrium.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब देखो theta one-eighty पर क्या होता है। Loop वहाँ से शुरू होती है। Torque zero है — यह equilibrium में होना चाहिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు theta one-eighty వద్ద ఏమి జరుగుతుందో చూడండి. Loop అక్కడ నుండి మొదలవుతుంది. Torque zero — అది equilibrium లో ఉండాలి.

tts_sentences.text_en: A tiny nudge — and the torque points away from theta one-eighty, not toward it. The loop flips all the way through, settling at theta zero.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक छोटा nudge — और torque theta one-eighty से दूर point करता है, उसकी तरफ नहीं। Loop पूरी तरह flip होकर theta zero पर settle हो जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక చిన్న nudge — మరియు torque theta one-eighty నుండి దూరంగా point అవుతుంది, దాని వైపు కాదు. Loop పూర్తిగా flip అయి theta zero వద్ద settle అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Like a pencil balanced on its tip. Mathematically possible, practically impossible. Real loops always settle at the stable pose.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Tip पर balanced pencil की तरह। Mathematically possible, practically impossible। Real loops हमेशा stable pose पर settle होती हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: దాని tip పై balanced pencil లాంటిది. Mathematically possible, practically impossible. Real loops ఎప్పుడూ stable pose వద్ద settle అవుతాయి.

STATE_10.title: A current loop IS a bar magnet — same μ, same τ

scene_composition.text: A current loop IS a bar magnet

Watch — loop fades, bar magnet appears.

μ vector stays put.
τ vector stays put.
The physics is identical.

This is why:
• galvanometer needles deflect
• electric motors spin
• MRI scanners flip protons

All the same law: τ = μ × B.
Next state — your turn. Drag the sliders.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch closely. The loop fades. A bar magnet appears with the same mu direction.
tts_sentences.text_hi: ध्यान से देखो। Loop fade हो जाती है। Same mu direction के साथ एक bar magnet appear होती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: శ్రద్ధగా చూడండి. Loop fade అవుతుంది. Same mu direction తో ఒక bar magnet appear అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Torque on the bar magnet is identical to torque on the loop. The two are physically the same object.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Bar magnet पर torque, loop पर torque के identical है। दोनों physically same object हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: Bar magnet పై torque, loop పై torque తో identical గా ఉంటుంది. రెండూ physically same object.

tts_sentences.text_en: This single equation explains the galvanometer needle, the electric motor, and even how MRI scanners flip the protons in your body.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह single equation galvanometer needle, electric motor, और यहाँ तक कि MRI scanners तुम्हारे body में protons को कैसे flip करते हैं — सब explain करती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఈ single equation galvanometer needle, electric motor, మరియు MRI scanners మీ body లో protons ని ఎలా flip చేస్తాయో కూడా explain చేస్తుంది.

STATE_11.title: Your turn — drag N, I, B, θ and watch τ respond

scene_composition.text: Sandbox — drag the four sliders

τ = N I A B sin θ

N doubles → τ doubles
I doubles → τ doubles
B doubles → τ doubles
θ moves → τ traces a sine

Try these:
1. Set θ = 90°, vary I — watch τ rise.
2. Max out I and B — sweep θ. Where is τ biggest?
3. Set θ = 0°. Move every other slider. τ stays zero. Why?

tts_sentences.text_en: Your turn. Four sliders below — turns N, current I, magnetic field B, and theta. Drag them. Watch the torque arrow respond in real time.
tts_sentences.text_hi: तुम्हारी बारी। नीचे चार sliders — turns N, current I, magnetic field B, और theta। उन्हें drag करो। Torque arrow को real time में respond करते देखो।
tts_sentences.text_te: మీ వంతు. కింద నాలుగు sliders — turns N, current I, magnetic field B, మరియు theta. వాటిని drag చేయండి. Torque arrow real time లో respond అవడం చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Try the three challenges on the right. Each one tests one piece of the equation. When you finish all three, you understand torque on a current loop.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दाएं तरफ तीन challenges try करो। हर एक equation का एक piece test करता है। जब तीनों खत्म कर लो, तब तुम current loop पर torque समझ गए।
tts_sentences.text_te: Right వైపు మూడు challenges try చేయండి. ప్రతి ఒక్కటి equation లో ఒక piece test చేస్తుంది. మూడూ పూర్తి చేసినప్పుడు, మీకు current loop పై torque అర్థమైనట్టే.

epic_c_branches.misconception: The magnetic force does work on the current loop — it speeds up the rotation, so KE rises.
trigger_phrases: magnetic force speeds up the loop
B does work on the rotating loop
loop rotation gains energy from B

epic_c_branches.misconception: A rotating loop must have a net force pushing it sideways — rotation cannot happen without translation force.
trigger_phrases: net force on the loop is nonzero
ΣF cannot be zero while spinning

epic_c_branches.misconception: Torque is maximum when μ is parallel to B — alignment means maximum effect.
trigger_phrases: torque max when mu parallel to B
tau is largest when mu aligned

epic_c_branches.misconception: The right-hand rule for μ is the same as the right-hand rule for the magnetic field around a current — fingers along the current, thumb along μ.
trigger_phrases: mu direction by RHR for wire
RHR for mu same as B around wire

STATE_1.label: Setup — loop at θ = 90°, no motion
STATE_1.caption: Rectangular loop in uniform B. θ = 90° — loop face parallel to the field axis. No force vectors shown yet.

STATE_2.label: F₁ on the left side — RHR reveals F₁ out of page
STATE_2.caption: Left side: current up, B right. RHR → F₁ INTO page. Magnitude I·L·B.

STATE_3.label: F₂ on the right — anti-parallel — ΣF = 0
STATE_3.caption: Right side: current down, B right. RHR → F₂ OUT of page. F₁ + F₂ = 0. Loop will not translate.

STATE_4.label: Torque emerges — couple → rotation about vertical axis
STATE_4.caption: ΣF = 0 yet Στ ≠ 0. The couple rotates the loop about the vertical axis. τ-arrow appears along the rotation axis.

STATE_5.label: μ vector through loop face — observe-only
STATE_5.caption: μ = NIA. Direction: perpendicular to loop face, RHR (fingers curl with current, thumb gives μ). Observe-only — interactivity is reserved for STATE_11.

STATE_6.label: θ sweep 0→180° — τ traces a sine, no sliders
STATE_6.caption: θ animates 0 → 180° and back on a 10-second cycle. τ-arrow scales with sin θ. No sliders.

STATE_7.label: Frozen at θ = 90° — predict the max angle
STATE_7.caption: Loop pinned at θ = 90°. Wrong-belief prompt and answer reveal via reveal_at_tts_id.

STATE_8.label: Stable equilibrium — small oscillation around θ = 0°
STATE_8.caption: Loop oscillates back and forth around θ = 0°. Restoring torque visible.

STATE_9.label: Unstable equilibrium — loop flips from θ = 170° through 180° → 360°
STATE_9.caption: Loop starts at θ = 170° (just past unstable). Torque drives it past 180° all the way to θ = 360° (= 0°).

STATE_10.label: Loop ↔ bar magnet swap at θ = 60°
STATE_10.caption: Loop fades, bar magnet appears with same μ direction. τ unchanged.

STATE_11.label: Interactive sandbox — drag N, I, B, θ
STATE_11.caption: Sliders control N, I, B, θ. τ and μ update on every drag.
